memoryArtifacts.headline: Alan II FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland (Seal)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Alan II FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland (Seal). The illustration is not credited. - The image has been scanned from Descriptive catalogue of impressions from ancient Scottish seals, royal, baronial ecclesiastical, and municipal, embracing a period from a.d. 1094 to the commonwealth, plate 3, figure 2, by Henry Laing, published in 1850. This book was published in Edinburgh, Scotland. The illustration is not credited.
Permission details
This image is in the public domain in the United States because it was first published outside the United States prior to January 1, 1923.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland於1140年出生在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利時，他的父親Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland34歲，母親Eschyna de Londiis de Molle33歲。他於1165年之前與Alesta nic Morggán of Mar在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1159年被列為2nd high steward of scotland。他去世於1204年8月24日在蘇格蘭艾斯爾凱爾享年64歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭雷福福爾佩斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland于1140年出生在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利时，他的父亲Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland34岁，母亲Eschyna de Londiis de Molle33岁。他于1165年之前与Alesta nic Morggán of Mar在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1159年被列为2nd high steward of scotland。他去世于1204年8月24日在苏格兰艾斯爾凱爾享年64岁，埋葬在苏格兰雷福福爾佩斯利。

nameMeanings.name: FitzWalter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , ‘son of Walter’.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Walter
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Alan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

en.lifeSummary: When Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland was born in 1140, in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, his father, Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland, was 34 and his mother, Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, was 33. He married Alesta nic Morggán of Mar before 1165, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. In 1159, at the age of 21, his occupation is listed as 2nd high steward of scotland. He died on 24 August 1204, in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, at the age of 64, and was buried in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland est né en 1140, à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse, son père, Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Alesta nic Morggán of Mar avant 1165, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. En 1159, à l’âge de 21 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est 2nd high steward of scotland. Il est décédé le 24 août 1204, à Kyle, Ayrshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Paisley, Renfrewshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland 1140 in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland 34 und seine Mutter Eschyna de Londiis de Molle 33. Er hat vor 1165 in Scotland Alesta nic Morggán of Mar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. 1159, als er 21 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als 2nd high steward of scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 24. August 1204 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland und wurde in Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland è nato il nel 1140 nel Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Alesta nic Morggán of Mar prima del 1165, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Nel 1159, all'età di 21 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come 2nd high steward of scotland. Morì il 24 agosto 1204, nel Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotlandが1140年にスコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーで生まれた時，父Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotlandは34歳，母Eschyna de Londiis de Molleは33歳であった。1165年以前にスコットランドでAlesta nic Morggán of Marと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1159年，21歳の時，職業は2nd high steward of scotlandと記録されている。彼は1204年8月24日にスコットランドエイルシュアイアクイルで64歳で死去し，スコットランドレンフリューシュアイアペイスリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland(이)가 1140년 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Eschyna de Londiis de Molle(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1165년 이전 스컷랜드에서 Alesta nic Morggán of Mar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1159년, 21세의 나이로 그의 직업은 2nd high steward of scotland이었다. 그는 1204년 8월 24일 스컷랜드 에이르실르 키일에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 렌프레w실르 파이슬리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland nasceu em 1140, em Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, seu pai, Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Alesta nic Morggán of Mar antes 1165, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Em 1159, com 21 anos, sua profissão aparece como 2nd high steward of scotland. Ele faleceu em 24 de agosto de 1204, em Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland родился в 1140 г. в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Alesta nic Morggán of Mar перед 1165 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. В 1159 году, в возрасте 21 год,  он работал по профессии 2nd high steward of scotland. Он умер 24 августа 1204 г. в местности кил, аиршир, Шотландия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности пеисли, ренфрюшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alan FitzWalter 2nd High Steward of Scotland nació en 1140, en Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland, su papá, Walter Fitz Alan 1st High Steward of Scotland, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Eschyna de Londiis de Molle, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Alesta nic Morggán of Mar antes de 1165, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. En 1159, a la edad de 21 años, su ocupación registrada era 2nd high steward of scotland. Murió el 24 de agosto de 1204, en Kyle, Ayrshire, Scotland, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Paisley, Renfrewshire, Scotland.
